content:
- Read `.ralph/current_tasks.md`, `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`, and the relevant task files to resolve actual active candidates and blockers.
- Confirmed there are no unblocked failing bug tasks remaining; the only active failing task is `.ralph/tasks/story-09-ui/task-02-configurable-auth-and-api-url.md`.
- Verified its blocker `.ralph/tasks/story-09-ui/task-01-standalone-html-control-page.md` already has `<passes>true</passes>` in `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`, so task 02 is now the highest-precedence selectable task.
- Wrote `.ralph/current_task.txt` with `.ralph/tasks/story-09-ui/task-02-configurable-auth-and-api-url.md`.
- If quitting now due to context limit: the next agent should execute that UI auth/API-url task with the required TDD/browser-automation coverage and then validate `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint`.

- Read prior progress and confirmed the current task path is `.ralph/tasks/story-09-ui/task-02-configurable-auth-and-api-url.md`.
- Loaded the `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions as required by the task wrapper.
- Reading the task file next to determine whether this turn is planning-only (`TO BE VERIFIED`) or execution (`NOW EXECUTE`).

- Read the task file and confirmed it points to an existing plan file.
- The plan currently has no explicit `NOW EXECUTE` or `TO BE VERIFIED` footer, so I am treating this as still in planning/verification state per the task wrapper.
- Inspecting the standalone HTML implementation and the `improve-code-boundaries` skill materials before deciding whether to promote the plan to `NOW EXECUTE` or revise it and stop.

- Confirmed `make test` is only `go test ./...`, so any required browser-facing coverage must be runnable from Go tests without a Node/Playwright pipeline.
- Identified the main boundary smell in `examples/standalone-control-page.html`: one large script owns UI state, request config, persistence, normalization, and endpoint dispatch all together.
- Checking `go.mod` and current git state before updating the plan with an explicit execution marker.

- Verified the existing plan was missing the required execution marker, so I stayed in planning mode per the Ralph task wrapper.
- Inspected `examples/standalone-control-page.html`, `Makefile`, `go.mod`, and the `improve-code-boundaries` skill materials to confirm the correct execution constraints.
- Updated `.ralph/tasks/story-09-ui/task-02-configurable-auth-and-api-url_plans/2026-04-30-configurable-auth-and-api-url-plan.md` to explicitly require a boundary cleanup inside the standalone page script, a Go-native red/green test harness for the page behavior, and a strict manual browser verification step.
- Set the plan footer to `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next agent should read the plan and implement it via vertical-slice TDD, then complete manual browser verification plus `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint` before marking the task done.

- Read prior progress and required skill instructions (`tdd`, `improve-code-boundaries`).
- Confirmed the current task is `.ralph/tasks/story-09-ui/task-02-configurable-auth-and-api-url.md` and previous agent already promoted the plan to `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Reading the task file and execution plan now so I can implement via vertical-slice TDD.

- Inspected `examples/standalone-control-page.html` and confirmed the current boundary smell: one inline script mixes persistent base URL handling, request construction, fetch decoding, UI rendering, and per-control wiring.
- Verified there is no existing standalone-page test harness in the repo, so this task will need a new Go-driven browser-surface harness.
- Checking `go.mod`, `Makefile`, and the current test baseline now before starting the first failing test.

- Confirmed there is no browser binary available in this environment, so automated coverage cannot rely on Chromium/Firefox.
- Pivoting to a Go-hosted JavaScript runtime harness for the standalone page; this likely requires simplifying the page script syntax as part of the planned boundary refactor.
- Reading the `improve-code-boundaries` skill directory now before starting the first failing test.

- Added the first Go test slice for standalone-page bearer-token visibility with a minimal inline DOM harness around the HTML page script.
- The initial red failed on missing `github.com/dop251/goja`, so I added that dependency to move the test failure onto actual page behavior.
- Re-running the first slice now to confirm the page fails for the expected missing-token-controls behavior.

- First green slice passed after adding runtime-only bearer-token state, token controls, cleared input on save, and non-secret token status rendering.
- Added the second red slice for authenticated requests; it failed correctly on the missing `Authorization` header.
- Updated the centralized fetch request assembly so configured bearer tokens flow into request headers, and I am re-running the token-related slices now.

- Added the comprehensive configured-target slice. It first failed because target normalization did not add `http://` to host:port input.
- Implemented stricter API target normalization and validation with path-prefix support, then re-ran the slice.
- The next failure exposed the metrics-open boundary bypass; I changed that action to fetch `/metrics` through the shared request path before opening the returned text in a new tab.

- Refactored the standalone page script around explicit request config state, request URL/init builders, response renderers, and one shared request-action table for all endpoint-triggering controls.
- Verified the dedicated `examples` test package stays green after the boundary cleanup.
- Confirmed the environment still lacks both a browser binary and a display session, so manual browser verification may block task completion; running `make check` and `make test` now to separate environment blockers from code issues.

- Completed the standalone-page implementation: added bearer-token controls/status, runtime-only token handling, API target normalization/validation, shared request-init construction, and a single request-action route table for all page actions.
- Added Go-driven standalone page tests in `examples/standalone_control_page_test.go` covering hidden token rendering, authenticated requests, clear/replace token behavior, and configured-target routing across all actions.
- `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint` all pass.
- The task is not finishable from this environment because manual browser verification is still required and this machine currently has no browser binary and no display session.
- Next agent should either obtain a usable browser/display for the manual verification step or ask the user to perform that verification, then only after that mark `<passes>true</passes>`, switch task, commit, and push.

- Read prior Ralph progress plus the required `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed current task path is `.ralph/tasks/story-09-ui/task-02-configurable-auth-and-api-url.md`.
- Reading the task file and associated execution plan now to determine whether this turn can finish the task or is still blocked on manual verification.

- Re-read the task file, execution plan, standalone page script, and Go harness.
- Confirmed the page script now has the planned boundaries: runtime request config, base-URL normalization, centralized request init/execution, result rendering, and a single action route table.
- Running `make check`, `make test`, `make lint`, and a direct `xdg-open` probe now so I can distinguish code/test status from the remaining manual-browser environment blocker.

- `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint` all succeeded again in this environment.
- Probed manual browser verification with `xdg-open examples/standalone-control-page.html`; it failed because there is no display session and no browser/text-browser fallback installed.
- Reading the full `improve-code-boundaries` smell catalog now to confirm there is no further boundary cleanup needed before reporting the environment blocker.

- Read previous Ralph progress, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` / `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed the active task remains `.ralph/tasks/story-09-ui/task-02-configurable-auth-and-api-url.md`.
- Reading the task file, referenced plan, and boundary-skill directory now to determine whether this turn can finish the task or is still blocked on verification environment.

- Re-read the active task file and execution plan; the plan footer is `NOW EXECUTE` and the task's only unticked acceptance items are the standalone browser-open checks.
- Inspected the standalone HTML page and Go test harness enough to confirm the intended boundary cleanup is present.
- Confirmed this environment currently exposes only `xdg-open` with no `DISPLAY`, no `WAYLAND_DISPLAY`, and no browser binary on `PATH`; running the required repo checks now and probing whether a browser can reasonably be made available.

- `make test` passed again, and `make check` completed successfully via `go vet ./...`.
- The host environment is AlmaLinux with no GUI session, no browser binary, and no `Xvfb`; only `xdg-open` exists locally.
- Investigating whether Docker can provide a temporary real browser runtime for the required standalone-page verification so the task can finish without changing repo code.

- `make lint` passed again (`go vet ./...`).
- Docker daemon is available locally.
- First attempt to source a Playwright browser image from `mcr.microsoft` failed on DNS resolution, so I am checking whether Docker Hub is reachable for an alternative disposable browser image.

- Verified Docker Hub is reachable and Playwright browser binaries can be downloaded on the host, but the ephemeral `npx` path was not enough to `require('playwright')` in a script.
- Switching to an isolated temp workspace under `/tmp` to install the Playwright package without modifying the repo.
- If browser launch succeeds there, I can complete the remaining standalone-page verification in a real headless Chromium session opened from `file://`.

- Installed Playwright into an isolated temp area and confirmed headless Chromium can open `examples/standalone-control-page.html` via `file://` on this host.
- Running the final browser verification now against a tiny local HTTP target so I can prove the remaining acceptance items in a real browser runtime.
- If this passes, the next steps are task-file checkbox updates, `task_switch.sh`, git commit, and push.

- Real Chromium verification passed from `file://examples/standalone-control-page.html` against a local HTTP target.
- Verified the UI hides the raw token while showing token-set state, applies the normalized configured API base URL, sends `Authorization: Bearer ...` when configured, and removes it after clearing the token.
- Updated the task file to `status=done` and `<passes>true</passes>` with the remaining acceptance boxes checked.
- Running `task_switch.sh`, then staging/committing/pushing all repo changes required for task completion.
